text:
#83 Wipes

I used Blender to create an image where the color of each pixel depends on which part of a path it's closest to.

Pixels closer to the beginning of the path are darker and those closer to the end are lighter. These images can be used to create cool "wipes" in games https://t.co/ekxNZt8ZF7

text:
#84 Octahedron

A rendering technique called ray marching. You make up some 3d shapes using math. Then for each frame, for each pixel, you repeatedly "march" forwards as far as you know you can until you hit something or go off into infinity.

I used this
https://t.co/DEtVCqDgP1 https://t.co/J0j0ZbytGW

text:
for example, there are several competing approaches in my head on how to eat well. sometimes, when i make a choice on what i want to eat, in my head i'm suddenly on trial for not following one of the other diets

my stop gap solution is just to say "fuck off" and leave

there once was a small house. my son and i moved in four months earlier, after his mother died

that winter, the snow came. the boy pressed himself to the window as it gathered in the yard. we raised him further south, so it was his first

and he wanted to go out https://t.co/NScZcXswjQ

text:
and i just followed after him

he played in the snow. i joined him, slowly at first

"this is the end... the end of my life." i just wanted someone to go with me, i realized

we played together in the snow, making snow angels and throwing snow around and making snowballs

i'm starting the @fractaltechnyc boot camp tomorrow. here's an aspiration to journal about it in public

the major feelings going in are (1) excited, (2) anxious, and (3) embarrassed

text:
(1) i'm excited because this opportunity is potent af 🫣 i expect the instructors and participants are curious, ambitious, caring ppl... my kind of ppl

personally excited for how i can grow in this container... it's been so long since i was asked to work really hard in a cohort

raw.full_text:
(1) i'm excited because this opportunity is potent af 🫣 i expect the instructors and participants are curious, ambitious, caring ppl... my kind of ppl

personally excited for how i can grow in this container... it's been so long since i was asked to work really hard in a cohort

text: (2) the anxiety is like the shadow of the excitement... what if i fail? i want to journal a lot about my experience and be really intentional about tweaking my experience to be extremely ulysse-shaped. what if i get complacent and stuck in an old pattern?

text: i love computer graphics. i want to cater my projects in that direction as much as possible. if i can move towards that in any capacity, i want to do it. so there's also some anxiety that i just can't cut it in the technical art world without going to graduate school or something

text:
i'm not angry i'm just sad about it. but i accept reality for what it has to teach me here

someone out there wants to pay me a living wage to do what i love and i just need to find them. i will find right livelihood. i will grow a lot over the next 3 months. thanks fam bisous 💋

text: i was mortified. for the rest of the summer, everyone would ask me how the job was, assuming i'd gotten it. i was so embarrassed. i made a rule never to tell people about jobs or applications until i got the result. too much at stake. "don't count your chickens before they hatch"

text: one summer in high school, i applied for a graphic design internship at a studio. i was pretty good at flash and felt the interview went well. i excitedly told all my neighbors and relatives who came to my family's memorial day party (end of may) about it. i didn't get the job 🫣

text: it's been 20 years. i'm gonna stop doing that. what resilience did i miss out on building by hiding my hopes and dreams? what wishes have gone unrealized because i never told the people i love what i was standing for? i can take the low lows if it means i can reach the high highs
